heading: Gymea Bay vs Hacks Ferry — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gymea Bay, New South Wales and Hacks Ferry, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gymea Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Hacks Ferry (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gymea Bay or Hacks Ferry safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gymea Bay has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Hacks Ferry scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gymea Bay scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Hacks Ferry scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gymea Bay or Hacks Ferry?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gymea Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.23% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
